domain-quick-start.header.title: Quick start guide
domain-quick-start.header.description: Your website or app is now behind Cloudflare. Requests to  come to Cloudflare first, so we can stop attacks and speed up your web traffic.
domain-quick-start.sections.plan.header: Get more out of your  plan
domain-quick-start.sections.plan.descrip: Several features are on by default, including CDN/caching, DDoS protection, SSL/TLS, and WAF. Explore other features included in your plan below and in the menu.
domain-quick-start.sections.plan.informa: These features are generally low risk, but we recommend reviewing the documentation (look for the <0>book icon</0>) for limitations and edge cases. If you notice any issues, deactivate one feature at a time to troubleshoot.
domain-quick-start.sections.plan.tabs.se: Activate one-click features that mitigate threats we find in your traffic. Find more options under <0>Security</0>.
domain-quick-start.sections.plan.tabs.pe: Activate one-click features that improve load times and reliability. Find more options under <0>Caching</0>, <1>Speed</1>, and <2>Traffic</2>.
domain-quick-start.sections.messages.max: You have used all available rules for your  plan. <0>Upgrade</0> to get more rules to strengthen your security posture.
domain-quick-start.sections.messages.rul: This rule is already deployed. Edit in <0>security rules</0>.
domain-quick-start.sections.configuratio: Feature
Activate?
Activate
You don't have permission to use this feature. To get permission, please contact your account administrator.
Always use HTTPS
Protect your users and data, and avoid browser security warnings by redirecting all HTTP (unencrypted) requests to the HTTPS (encrypted) equivalent.
Automatic HTTPS Rewrites
Increase security and prevent “mixed content” errors and by automatically rewriting linked URLs from HTTP (unencrypted) to HTTPS (encrypted) if available.
Bot Fight Mode
Challenge known bots that may carry out attacks. Defend against threats like content scraping, click fraud, and credential stuffing by challenging simple bots with known traffic signatures.
Super Bot Fight Mode
Block AI bots and challenge complex bots using machine learning and prevent threats like content scraping using the standard configuration of Super Bot Fight Mode.
Block AI bots and challenge bots from any source that may carry out attacks like content scraping and credential stuffing using the standard configuration of Super Bot Fight Mode.
<0>Note:</0> If you customize settings individually, then this standard configuration will display as disabled even if some of the individual settings are still enabled.
Client-side security
Ensure the safety and privacy of your visitors by monitoring third-party scripts, connections, and cookies loaded in web browsers.
Cloudflare Managed Ruleset for WAF
Minimize security risk from new attack types as they are identified by deploying the constantly updated, Cloudflare Managed Ruleset.
Leaked credentials mitigation
Turn on leaked credentials detection and deploy a rate limiting rule to meter the number of requests from IP addresses attempting to use leaked or stolen passwords for 10 seconds.
OWASP Core Ruleset for WAF
Block common, yet critical attack types like cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection (SQLi) by deploying the OWASP Core Ruleset.
WAF Attack Score Lite
Block previously unknown threats scored as attacks by creating a custom rule based on WAF Attack Score Lite's machine learning.
Always Online™
Allow visitors to access your domain even when your origin server is unavailable by serving limited copies of pages from the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine.
By enabling, you agree to the <0>Supplemental Terms </0> for Always Online™.
Speed optimizations
Improve page load times by enabling tools like most likely next page prefetch and resumed connections for returning visitors.
Improve page load times by enabling tools like automatic image compression, prioritized loading of key site assets, and most likely next page prefetch.
domain-quick-start.sections.customizatio: How Cloudflare products interact with your HTTP traffic
When a request for  hits the Cloudflare network, it is processed in phases. This sequence may impact how you configure our products or specific rules within them.
Modify HTTP requests and responses, perform URL redirects, configure settings, and trigger actions for matching requests.
Simulate an HTTP request to understand the impact of your Cloudflare configurations.
domain-quick-start.sections.monitor.head: Monitor traffic and threats
View web page performance as experienced by your visitors with real-user measurements.
domain-quick-start.sections.monitor.obse: Find ways to improve performance by running tests with synthetic data.
domain-quick-start.sections.monitor.anal: Review requests processed by Cloudflare to assess trends and tune your configuration.
domain-quick-start.sections.monitor.secu: Review threat indicators such as traffic spikes and likelihood of attackers or bots.
domain-quick-start.sections.notification: Set up alerts for billing, DDoS attacks, origin health, and more via email, Webhooks, or PagerDuty.
